items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin holds $62,600 as the Iran conflict reignites and CPI looms
items.description: Trump reinstated the Hormuz blockade, sending oil higher and rate-hike bets up, reversing the peace trade that helped bitcoin recover in early July. Today's inflation print is the next test.

items.title: U.S. government moves $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime
items.description: Coins from the Farace and BTC-e seizures moved through fresh wallets before landing on the exchange, despite president Donald Trump's previous no-sell reserve order.

items.title: Solo bitcoin miner makes $200,000 using $150 equipment
items.description: Solo Bitcoin mining has surged, with 24 blocks found in the past 12 months, a 41% increase year over year.

items.title: Bitcoin slips as traders lift July Fed rate hike bets ahead of Inflation report
items.description: Major cryptocurrencies have dropped by 2% or more in 24 hours as traders boosted bets of a July Fed rate hike.

items.title: Binance.US CEO says exchange is rebuilding, eyes return to 20% U.S. market share
items.description: Binance.US is betting ultra-low fees, new regulated products and deeper liquidity will help it regain customers after two years of regulatory setbacks.

items.title: Franklin Crypto CIO says crypto prices are disconnected from fundamentals
items.description: Institutional adoption of crypto is accelerating even as digital asset prices fail to reflect the industry's strongest fundamentals in years, Seth Ginns said.

items.title: TeraWulf CEO: 'Not All Megawatts Are Created Equally' in AI Race
items.description: TeraWulf says its $19 billion AI hosting agreement with Anthropic underscores its transformation from a Bitcoin miner into an AI infrastructure company.

items.title: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
items.description: Discussion among Democrats have fixed on the president's personal gains when hashing out the crypto market structure bill's ethics provision.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt to report for military training: Report
items.description: Witt’s departure comes during a critical moment for the CLARITY Act, with deputy director Harry Jung expected to assume his responsibilities.

items.title: US government moves $297M in seized Bitcoin, Ether to Coinbase Prime
items.description: The transfers have raised questions over Trump’s Bitcoin reserve pledge, though deposits to Coinbase Prime do not confirm an impending sale.

items.title: Thai scammer's $122M wallet, Japan embraces crypto credit: Asia Express
items.description: Bitcoin backed mortgages and stablecoin yield are hot topics in Japan right now, as Hyundai experiments with Avalanche for global stablecoin transfers. Asia Express

items.title: CLARITY Act gains second law enforcement endorsement before Senate push
items.description: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association expressed support for the CLARITY Act but urged changes to the legislation’s language.

items.title: Global law firm launches MiCA compliance tool as crypto companies navigate new EU rules
items.description: Reed Smith’s Aquarius platform automates regulatory filings and legal workflows as demand for MiCA compliance tools grows across Europe.

items.title: Bolivia mulls recognizing USDT as payment currency amid dollar shortage
items.description: As foreign currency reserves remain under pressure, Bolivia is considering a framework to let the world’s largest stablecoin be used for payments, savings and trade.

items.title: Hyundai completes USDT treasury settlement pilot between US and Mexico
items.description: Hyundai completed a proof-of-concept using Tether's USDT to settle a cross-border treasury transfer between its US and Mexican subsidiaries, highlighting growing enterprise interest in stablecoin-based payments.

items.title: Oil jumps 3% as U.S.-Iran ceasefire fractures; Trump's Hormuz toll plans reignite supply fears
items.description: Oil prices rose on Tuesday after U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to impose shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: U.S. targets Iranian military assets in another round of strikes as Tehran hits Gulf states
items.description: The U.S. has completed a third consecutive night of strikes against Iran, as Tehran targets U.S. military facilities in the region.

items.title: Taiwan's second-largest chipmaker starts mass production in Singapore; Citi sees improving outlook
items.description: Taiwan’s UMC has kicked off mass production of silicon photonics wafers at its Singapore facility amid positive Citi forecast.

items.title: China exports in June rise at fastest pace since 2021 as AI boom, tariff rush lift trade
items.description: China's exports to the U.S. jumped around 14% last month while imports grew 26%, according to CNBC calculation of the official data.

items.title: Trump's Hormuz toll plans bring oil supply risks back in spotlight
items.description: While the proposed levy would raise shipping costs, an escalating conflict could remove barrels from the market altogether.

items.title: Trump proposes 20% toll on cargo through Strait of Hormuz; restarts Iran blockade
items.description: The Strait of Hormuz is a major oil shipping route and the epicenter of the U.S. and Israel's ongoing war with Iran.

items.title: South Carolina governor taps Lindsey Graham's sister to serve as interim senator
items.description: The death of Sen. Lindsey Graham ignited a scramble among South Carolina Republicans to prepare for a special primary election to replace him on the GOP ticket.

items.title: Japan’s bond market is back in play after decades in the wilderness
items.description: JGBs have been selling off amid policy normalization and concerns over Tokyo's spending plans, but experts say that they deserve another look from investors.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8IExFvX3LVo! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: The U.S. is maxing out its strategic oil reserves as Trump vows to control the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve stockpiles are precariously low and have been hit by major equipment failures, leaks and spills, according to a government report.

items.title: Sandisk’s stock is plunging, but some analysts are getting even more bullish
items.description: One new stock-price target implies nearly 85% upside from current levels.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
items.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

items.title: Why a borrowing binge by investors is a warning sign for the stock market
items.description: Investors are increasingly borrowing to buy stocks, a reflection of greed in the stock market as they seek to amplify returns with margin debt. The growing pile of borrowed money has some on Wall Street nervous.

items.title: SpaceX’s stock threatens to fall below the IPO price. Do investors face a ‘crisis’ if it does?
items.description: The company has shed more than $800 billion in market value from its $2.67 trillion peak in less than a month.

items.title: Lindsey Graham died from a rare heart condition. Here’s how to know if you’re at risk.
items.description: Aortic dissections are extremely rare; however, people with a family history or who have been diagnosed with certain genetic syndromes have an elevated risk

items.title: You don’t have to be rich to be financially independent. Here’s how to take control of your money.
items.description: Relying on a high salary or a hot stock is a dangerous trap. Why the most secure people plan for sudden crisis.

- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: India presses banks to court diaspora’s deposits as rupee weakens
items.description: Foreign exchange reserves have dwindled by $54bn since start of Iran war

items.title: How QE changed banks, and why that makes QT dangerous
items.description: An interview with Jill Cetina

items.title: US launches air strikes as Iran targets tankers with cruise missiles
items.description: Donald Trump demands 20% fee on cargoes passing through Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Serbia’s Vučić urges EU to admit Western Balkan states as a group
items.description: Also in this newsletter: the EU is not doing enough to shield farmers, says Spain’s agriculture minister

items.title: You should be glad your boss is so well paid
items.description: Because it probably means you’re being paid well too

items.title: FirstFT: US retaliates after Iran hit tankers with missiles
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Nvidia halves Asia buyer list and Rothschild’s UK investment bank

items.title: China’s growth: five things to watch as economic pressure mounts
items.description: Monthly indicators show weakness in retail sales and investment ahead of second-quarter figure

items.title: The political fast lane keeps making the same scandal mistake
items.description: A story might be reported to no great acclaim for months before it finally blows up in the public consciousness

items.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Fee Could Double the Cost of Shipping
items.description: The potential expense of a 20 percent charge to move oil and other goods through the strait has stirred concern among shippers.

items.title: Trump Administration Is Snapping Up Stakes in Private Companies. Could A.I. Be Next?
items.description: Some tech executives are nervous that the administration’s recent scrutiny of artificial intelligence models could be a prelude to a demand for an ownership stake.

items.title: The Fed’s Christopher Waller Warns of Higher Rates Soon if Inflation Stays High
items.description: Christopher J. Waller, a governor at the Federal Reserve, said he needed to see several months of lower inflation data to feel confident about the outlook.

items.title: Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger Is Challenged in Court by California and 11 Other States
items.description: The lawsuit, filed by California and 11 other states, argues that the deal would harm movie theaters and damage America’s entertainment industry.

items.title: U.S. Electric Vehicle Sales are Down but E.V. Chargers are Booming
items.description: Electric vehicle chargers are proliferating in Southern states as fast food restaurants, stores and other businesses try to lure customers.

items.title: Will Oil Prices Rise as the US-Iran Truce Frays? The Answer Lies With China.
items.description: For decades, OPEC influenced the market by how much oil it produced. But China, the largest importer, is demonstrating its remarkable power over prices.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Traffic Falls to Lowest Point in a Month After Strikes
items.description: Shipping activity in the critical waterway has plunged after Iran attacked a vessel, setting off U.S. strikes. President Trump said he would reinstate a blockade of Iranian ports.

items.title: Wall Street’s Big Week for Earnings and Economic Data
items.description: Earnings season kicks off in earnest on Tuesday with big banks up first. Corporate America faces a high bar to beat investor expectations.
